texts.full_text: multiple electronic elements 220—it also incorporates an encapsulant material 230. The electronic elements 220 are disposed on the surface 212 of PCB 210 and electrically connected to it via conductive traces or solder joints, similar to structure 100. However, in this design (structure 200), an encapsulant material 230 is applied over electronic elements 220. The purpose of the encapsulant 230 is multifaceted: - Protection: It protects sensitive components from environmental factors such as moisture and dust. - Mechanical Stability: By securing the position of each component, it enhances mechanical stability during handling or operation in harsh environments. - Thermal Management: Some encapsulants are designed with thermal conductivity properties to facilitate heat dissipation away from critical electronic elements. Despite these advantages, conventional package structures like 200 still face challenges. For instance: - The process of applying the encapsulant can be complex and may introduce voids or bubbles that could compromise component protection. - Heat management remains a challenge; while some materials help dissipate heat more effectively than others, achieving uniform thermal dissipation across all components is difficult. Moreover, as electronic devices become increasingly miniaturized with higher power densities, there's an increasing demand for package

texts.full_text: ndex of clinical suspicion to make an early diagnosis when myxoedema coma is present. Mortality has fallen from 80% to 20%--40% in treated individuals partly due to increased awareness of physicians, improved diagnostic testing, and advances in intensive care \[[@B3]\]. However, these statistics are based on developed countries and Dutta and colleagues raise a pertinent point in highlighting the differences in the developing world, where ready access to laboratory tests is not always possible and education for the primary physician, who does not have to deal with large numbers of thyroid conditions, remains important. It is evident that these patients need to be treated in an intensive care setting with close monitoring of their cardiovascular status. Ventilatory support is often needed because of decreased level of consciousness, respiratory depression secondary to drugs, underlying pneumonia, or sometimes macroglossia or myxoedema of the larynx resulting in airway obstruction \[[@B3]\]. Hypothermia, besides conventional treatment with warm blankets and fluids, requires replacement with thyroid hormones to normalise thermoregulation. There is consensus that all patients should be given glucocorticoids as these patients may have coexistent adrenal insufficiency; thyroid hormone replacement may result in increased metabolism of cortisol, thereby precipitating adrenal crisis. However, controversy regarding optimal replacement regimens persists due to the paucity of large clinical trials \[[@B6]-[@B10]\]. Where Youll Live Missoula is a sophisticated university town of 65,000 halfway between Glacier and Yellowstone National Parks. The area offers unlimited recreational opportunities like skiing, paddling, fishing and hiking. Its numerous cultural amenities, including a symphony and active theater, fuel a vibrant arts scene. Missoula is a family-oriented community with great public schools, universities and social activities year-round. Western Montanas summers consist of long, adventure-inspiring days and starry nights, and its winters are mild. Who Youll Work For Providence is a nationally recognized, comprehensive health care organization spanning seven states with a universal mission to provide compassionate care to all who need its services, especially the poor and vulnerable. COBased on California's Brown Act (Government Code section 54950 et seq.), which governs open meetings and public business, it is our opinion that such an ordinance would be unenforceable. The Brown Act requires cities to conduct most of their deliberations in open sessions where the public can attend and observe. While closed sessions are permitted under certain circumstances (e.g., for discussions involving personnel matters or legal advice), any actions taken during these closed sessions must still ultimately occur publicly, with proper notice given beforehand. An ordinance making it a crime to disclose what was discussed in a properly held closed session would effectively chill public discussion and discourage citizens from reporting violations of the Brown Act. This is contrary to the spirit and purpose of open government laws like the Brown Act. Therefore, we conclude that such an ordinance could not be enforced as it violates principles of op

The pipeline can be summarized like this : sc.textFile("gs://path/*.json")\ .map(lambda row: json.loads(row))\ .map(toKvPair)\ .groupByKey().take(10) [...] later processing on collections and output to GCS. This computation over the elements of collections is not associative, each element is sorted in it's keyspace. When run on 10GB of data, it's completed without any issue. However when I run it on the full dataset, it fails all the time with this logs in the containers: 15/11/04 16:08:07 WARN org.apache.spark.scheduler.cluster.YarnSchedulerBackend$YarnSchedulerEndpoint: ApplicationMaster has disassociated: xxxxxxxxxxx 15/11/04 16:08:07 ERROR org.apache.spark.scheduler.cluster.YarnClientSchedulerBackend: Yarn application has already exited with state FIt seems like you are facing an issue while processing a large dataset in PySpark on YARN mode, and the job is failing due to some errors related to disassociation of ApplicationMaster and yarn application exit status 'F'. Here's what I would suggest: 1. Increase Memory: Since you're dealing with 2TB of data, it might be a memory-intensive operation for Spark. Try increasing the executor/driver memory allocated in your YARN configuration or using dynamic allocation to allow more resources when needed during processing. 2. texts.full_text: y activists. While considering how work should be recorded and integrated into archives, we also critically examine the problematic legacy of archival processes that don’t make room for divergent experiences of lived history. Through this book we hope to inspire activists and challenge information professionals to think concretely about how archives can both tell history of grassroots organizing and contribute to ongoing social movement struggles. Jen Hoyer has been involved as a volunteer at Interference Archive since 2013, working on exhibitions, cataloging, and more. Jen loves working through how archives can help people understand themselves and their place in the world. Her background as an information worker spans public, school and special libraries in Canada, and she currently teaches K-12 students how think critically about the world around them through the lens of the local history archive at Brooklyn Public Library. Her writing on libraries, archives, and the way these institutions intersect with communities has appeared in Reference Services Review, Archival Science, Radical Teacher, and a forthcoming issue of the Journal of Critical Library and Information Studies, as well as chapters in Informed Agitation: Library and Information Skills in Social Justice Movements and Beyond (Library Juice Press, 2014) and Public Libraries and Resilient Cities (ALA Editions, 2013). She is the Innovations in Practice section editor for Partnership Journal: The Canadian Journal of Library and Information Practice and Research. texts.full_text: the "Game" back in "MMORPG"Preview based on BetaArticle by Scott Clark A year or two ago, I had an opportunity to beta test for a high profile MMO being developed in the classic "EQ" style. I didn't last long. At some point my defection earned me a truly "epic" exit survey inquiring as to why I hadabandoned testing...evidently, this was a common problem. After four pages of answering the same basic question over and over again, withnooption that fit what I really wanted to say, I finally reached the last page which asked for any additional comments. It was there that I let loose with my true feelings about the game. I told them in no uncertain terms that their game sucked. The response from the development team was swift and harsh. They accused me of being a troll, of not understanding thegame, and of not giving it a fair chance. But here's the thing: I did understand the game. I played it for weeks, leveled multiple characters, and explored every corner of the world they had available at the time. And you know what? It still sucked. So when I heard that NCsoft was developing a new MMO called Tabula Rasa, I was skeptical. Another "EQ" style game? Pass. But then I saw some early screenshots and read about the game's unique features, and I started to get interested. This wasn't just another fantasy MMO with elves and dwarves and dragons. This was a sci-fi MMO with a compelling story, innovative gameplay mechanics, and a fresh perspective on the genre. Fast forward to now, and I've been playing Tabula Rasa in beta for a few weeks. texts.full_text: of the Most Holy Body and Blood of Christ." This celebration highlights one of Catholicism’s most central mysteries—the Real Presence of Jesus Christ in the Eucharist. It invites us to reflect deeply on what it means for our faith that we believe not only do bread and wine become transformed into His body and blood, but also how this sacrament nourishes both individuals spiritually as well as binds together communities. The feast has a rich history dating back centuries within Christianity; however, its formal establishment in the liturgical calendar came about through an interesting series of events. In 1264 Pope Urban IV instituted Corpus Christi at the suggestion of St. Thomas Aquinas who composed some beautiful hymns for this day including "Pange Lingua" and “Tantum Ergo,” which are still sung during Eucharistic adoration today. In many parts of the world, particularly in Europe but also increasingly elsewhere around globe where Catholicism is practiced robustly enough to support such activities—Corpus Christi processions take place. texts.full_text: agnetic tape to a slave magnetic tape. In the prior art method, a magnetic tape which uses chromium dioxide CrO.sub.2 as a magnetic material is used as a slave magnetic tape. In this case, the Curie temperature of the chromium dioxide is about 130.degree.C which is relatively low and the coercive force of the chromium dioxide greatly depends upon the temperature. The coercive force of the chromium dioxide is about 500 O.sub.e at room temperature, but decreases as the chromium dioxide is heated. When the chromium dioxide is heated to about 130.degree.C, its coercive force becomes zero. When using a slave tape having the characteristics mentioned above, it is desirable to use a master tape with magnetic material having a higher Curie temperature than that of the slave tape and when the slave tape is heated to about 130.degree.C, and thereafter is cooled so that information signals recorded on the master tape will be transferred to the slave tape. In one of the embodiments of prior application, Ser. No. 444,571, when the temperature of the slave tape deviates from a reference temperature (about 130.degree.C), a heating device such as a lamp is controlled in brightness as a function of the difference between the temperature of the slave tape and the reference temperature so as to make the temperature difference substantially zero. However, this requires a long interval of time and thus the speed of response is slow

He made a second run for the seat in 1929, and narrowly lost to Bayley. In the 1931 election he contested Oxley for a third time, this time successfully. texts.full_text: CFCs) have little toxicity and are, in many cases, non-flammable and chemically stable. Various CFCs having different boiling points are available. By virtue of such properties, 1,1,2-trichloro-1,2,2-trifluoroethane (R113) is used as a solvent or a blowing agent; trichloro-1,2,2-trifluoroethane (R11) is used as a refrigerant; and dichlorodifluoromethane (R12) is used as a refrigerant and a fire extinguishing agent. R11 and R12, however, have a high ozone depletion potential and, therefore, their use is now restricted under the Montreal Protocol. This protocol restricts the production and use of CFCs and is the cornerstone of the worldwide effort to protect the ozone layer. The ozone layer protects the earth from the harmful effects of the sun's ultraviolet radiation. The depletion of the ozone layer is caused by the release of CFCs and other ozone depleting substances (ODS) into the atmosphere. These substances are chemicAlly stable and do not decompose in the atmosphere. However, once in the atmosphere, CFCs and other ODS are transported by the atmosphere's winds and eventually reach the ozone layer. texts.full_text: al area covered by Dong Phi was approximately 1,050 square kilometers as per data available from that period. Dong Phu is known for its diverse landscapes and rich cultural heritage influenced significantly during various historical periods including French colonization when it served strategic military purposes due to the presence of important infrastructure like railways. Today's economy in Đồng Xuân District relies heavily on agriculture, with rice being a major crop alongside other staples such as rubber trees which have been increasingly cultivated over recent decades. The district is divided into 14 communes (xã) and one township (thị trấn), each contributing to the local administration. Infrastructure development has gradually improved connectivity within Đồng Phú, enhancing trade with neighboring districts in Bình Phước Province as well as other regions of Vietnam's Southeast. Culturally rich due its history involving different ethnic groups including Khmer and Hoa (Chinese-Vietnamese), Dong Phi hosts various cultural festivals that reflect this diversity. These celebrations are important for preserving local traditions, fostering community bonds among residents from varied backgrounds in the district. Education facilities have seen improvements over time with more schools being established to cater better educational needs of its growing population; however challenges remain regarding access and quality at higher levels due partly limited resources typical rural areas face compared urban centers. Overall Đồng Phú remains a vital part not just economically but also culturally within Bình Phước Province, contributing significantly towards the development goals set forth by regional authorities in Vietnam's broader socio-economic landscape. texts.full_text: e threateneHuman Health Threatened by Antibiotic Residues in Food The use of antibiotics as fluoroquinolones (FQs), sulfonamides, and tetracyclines is prevalent not only due to their broad-spectrum effectiveness but also because they address a range of bacterial infections. However, these compounds carry significant risks when used improperly or without adherence to withdrawal periods in livestock production. Potential Health Risks from Antibiotic Residues: 1. Direct Toxicity and Resistance: The presence of antibiotic residues can pose direct toxic effects on consumers if ingested through food products like milk, eggs, meat etc., especially for those with compromised immune systems or allergies. 2. Drug-Resistance Development: Continuous exposure to sub-lethal levels of antibiotics may promote the development of resistant strains among bacteria present in humans and animals alike. 3. Specific Side Effects: - have been associated with severe side effects as carcinogenicity, genetic mutations that can lead potentially cancerous changes at cellular levels; photosensitization which increases skin sensitivity to sunlight leading possibly sunburn or other dermatological conditions and allergic reactions. - The use of has been linked with adverse effects such as micturition issues, disrupting normal urinary functions potentially causing infections. They also affect hematopoiesis—the process by which blood cells are formed—leading to disorders in the production and function of these essential cellular components. - have been reported not only for their potential liver or kidney damage but they can adversely impact growth, especially concerning during developmental stages. Conclusion: The widespread use underscores a pressing need within agricultural practices to enforce strict regulations and adherence regarding the application of veterinary drugs in animal breeding operations. Given these significant implications on human health from antibiotic residues present due to improper management protocols or non-compliance with established guidelines for drug withdrawal periods, it is crucial that both regulatory bodies tighten their oversight mechanisms. Furthermore educating farmers about responsible use can also play a pivotal role alongside technological advances like developing alternative growth-promoting strategies which do not rely heavily upon antibiotics. texts.full_text: class of traces.' address: 'School of Mathematics and Statistics, University of New South Wales, Sydney, 2052, Australia.' author: - Fedor Sukochev - Alexandr Usachev - Dmitriy Zanin title: Dixmier traces generated by exponentiation invariant generalised limits --- *Keywords*: Dixmier traces, measurable elements, exponentiation invariant generalized limits, Lidskii formula. *2000 MSC*: Primary 58B34, 46L52 Introduction and Preliminaries ============================== In the framework of noncommutative geometry the Dixmier traces, originally introduced by J. Dixmier in [@D], have become an indispensable tool. These traces are defined via dilation invariant generalized restrictions on symmetric sequences. The theory of Dixmier traces is important in the analysis of noncommutative integration and has found important applications in many areas of physics, including the theory of operator ideals, the analysis of operator algebras, and noncommutative algebra. In this paper, we introduce a new class of singular positive traces on the ideal $\mathcal{M}_{1,\infty}$ of $M(M)$ generated by exponentiation invariant generalized constraints. Our methodology is rooted in the concept of constructing generalized constraints that possess a certain invariance property with regard to exponentiation. texts.full_text: speed, I also tried to increase the framerate as I've read, it improves the latency issue. In my code which uses the opencv library, I used the grab() and retrieve() commands to replace the read() command so that the frames from both cameras is grabbed at the nearest time possible however it didn't help much. Does anyone know any possible solutions? A: You need real camera synchronization which means using cameras that both have external trigger capability (it is like the "remote shutter" on consumer/DSLRs). What you do is feed a common trigger signal to both cameras and sync them like that down to the sub-ms level (the accuracy largely depends on the camera HW but is usually very good, usually in us). Frame synchronization is not implemented on the RPi camera so you can not sync two cameras for stereo (even though the v1 cam OmniVision sensor had a FREX pin which was kind of external trigger - but it was neither routed out of the sensor to the camera board nor implemented in the camera driver). What you are trying to do is is SW sync both raspi cams but both modules are in "free running" mode (they are streaming images at given FPS each continuously) and OpenCV only grabs the last frame that was taken which is far from being synchronous capture. Your frame offset may be as high as the frame rate is, but even at 90 FPS you still have some 10 ms difference which most likely is unacceptable for an UAV application. Should you have a static environment you would not need to sync the cameras at all, but you seem to know this :). There were community attempts to frame sync the raspi cam, search for "frame synchronization" on the raspberrypi.org forum if you are interested. texts.full_text: breakdown of the neurotransmitter, acetylcholine (ACh), in cholinergic synapses. In the brain, ACh subserves functions e.g. for attention, learning and memory. Central cholinergic neurons which innervate cortex and hippocampus are typically degenerating during Alzheimer's disease; this process is accompanied by attention and memory deficits in Alzheimer patients. In the present proposal, we suggest to investigate central cholinergic functions in genetically modified mice which are deficient for ACHE. These mice have body tremor and are prone to seizures, observations which are compatible with an excess of ACh due to lack of enzymatic breakdown. The first aim of our study is to quantify extracellular levels of ACh in the brain of AChE-deficient mice compared to heterozygous mice and control mice. For this purpose, we will use the microdialysis technique adapted to mice. We will also measure high-affinity choline uptake to determine possible compensatory changes of ACh turnover in cholinergic neurons. However, the fact that AChE-deficient mice are alive suggests that other pathways may e...exist to compensate for the lack of ACHE-mediated breakdown

texts.full_text: and neuronal size, rather than a loss of neurons. These morphometric changes are in turn suggestive of alterations in synaptic, dendritic and axonal organization, a view supported by immunocytochemical and ultrastructural findings. Pathology in subcortical structures is not well established, apart from dorsal thalamic nuclei, which are smaller and contain fewer neurons. Other cytoarchitectural features of schizophrenia which are often discussed, notably entorhinal cortex heterotopias and hippocampal neuronal disarray, remain to be confirmed. The phenotype of the affected neuronal and synaptic populations is uncertain. A case can be made for impairment of hippocampal and corticocortical excitatory pathways, but in general the relationship between neurochemical findings (which centre upon dopamine, 5-hydroxytryptamine, glutamate and GABA systems) and the neuropathology of schizophrenia is unclear. Gliosis is not an intrinsic feature; its absence supports, but does not prove, the prevailing hypothesis that schizophrenia is a disorder of prenatal neurodevelopment. The cognitive impairment which frequently accompanies schizophrenia is not due to Alzheimer's disease or any other recognized neurodegenerative disorder. Its basis is unknown. The series is directed by award winning TV director Mehreen Jabbar, is written by popular Urdu novelist Umera Ahmed and produced by Humayun Saeed and Abdullah Kadwani's production house 7th Sky Entertainment. It was also aired in India on Zindagi, premiering on 26 October 2015. She is the daughter of Hidayatullah (Shahid Naqvi), a wealthy businessman, and Amna (Lubna Aslam), a caring and supportive mother. Zara's brother, Jamaal (Muhammad Yasir), is also a part of their family and is always there to support Zara in her endeavors. As Zara begins her journey in the business world, she faces various challenges and obstacles. She meets Maleeha (Aamina Sheikh), a young and talented artist who becomes her close friend and confidant. Junaid (Adeel Hussain), a wealthy and influential man, also enters Zara's life and becomes her love interest.
